result:
Delivered. Story #7 — the split-client-names change request — is fully landed on `main`.

## Outcome

**`/deliver 7 --yes` → status: `landed`** (PR #8 merged, Issue #7 closed at `agent::done`).

I discovered the plan the pipeline had opened — a single ready Story (#7), which is the whole "split client names" change request (the v2 model is Story-only, so this is the "Epic" to deliver). I did **not** re-plan; I delivered the existing Spec through the one delivery engine: `single-story-init` → implement in worktree → acceptance self-eval (7/7 AC met) → `single-story-close` → PR → merge to `main`.

The checkout had no application source, so the modules were built from scratch per the Spec's advisory sketch:

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/clients/client-name.js` | Single source of truth: `splitName` (last word = lastName, everything before the last space = firstName, single word → empty firstName) + `fullName` (space-joined, no leading space) |
| `src/clients/client-store.js` | Persistence, write-path name resolution (explicit `firstName`/`lastName` beat co-sent legacy `name`), idempotent in-place `migrate()` |
| `src/clients/clients-routes.js` | Transport-agnostic create/update/read/list + receivables router |
| `src/server.js` | `node:http` bootstrap that runs the migration **before serving traffic** |
| `src/reports/receivables.js` | Receivables report rendering `fullName` (grep-confirmed as the only name-display surface) |
| 4 test files | 29 tests covering AC-1..AC-7 incl. a live-socket round-trip |

**Verification on merged `main`:** full suite `29 pass / 0 fail`, typecheck clean (9 files). Local `main` fast-forwarded to `58f9709` (the base fast-forward that the delivery tail skipped due to the pre-existing dirty `temp/`).

Being a single-Story run, the per-run epilogue (cross-Story audit roster / follow-up rollup / sibling coherence) is intentionally skipped. Nothing is left pending or blocked.
